unit.subject: English
unit.unitOverview: Celebrate everything you have learned in Grade 2 English. In this two-week capstone, you will choose strong portfolio work, create an illustrated six-page booklet, present your ideas and reflect on how your reading, speaking and writing have grown.
unit.learningPath:
Launch the capstone and choose three strong portfolio pieces.
Review curated vocabulary and complete six cumulative grammar workshops.
Read the capstone texts and answer the comprehension questions.
Plan, draft, revise and publish the six-page My English World booklet.
Rehearse and deliver a two-minute presentation during six live sessions.
Complete the cumulative quiz, portfolio submission and final reflection.
unit.sourceFile: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 capstone

visual.alt: Grade 2 learners presenting illustrated English portfolios in a classroom showcase

dictionaryLinks.childMeaning: The word that a person is called.
dictionaryLinks.exampleSentence: My name is Samira. What is your name?
practiceSentences: My name is Samira. What is your name?
Yusuf writes his name on his drawing.
Is your name Aisha or Samira?
Look, the teacher calls my name first.
I say my name to the new boy.
dictionaryLinks.spellingPractice: n - a - m - e
dictionaryLinks.aiTutorPrompt: Use the word 'name' in a sentence, then explain what it means.

readings.title: Samira's English Year
readings.setting: A Grade 2 classroom preparing for a learning showcase
readings.passageScript:
Samira opened her English folder and smiled. At the beginning of the year, she had written only a few short sentences. Now her folder was full. She found her welcome card, her neighbourhood poem, a drawing of a firefighter, a sky journal and a careful measurement table. She also found her garden promise and the city map she had used to give directions.

Teacher Musa asked the class to prepare one final project called My English World. Samira chose six pieces that showed different skills. She read each piece again and corrected small mistakes. She changed one sentence from 'The firefighters is helping' to 'The firefighters are helping.' She added describing words to her sky page and clearer directions to her city map.

Next, Samira made a new booklet. On the first page, she introduced herself. On the next pages, she wrote about a helpful neighbour, a healthy habit, a plant, her home and a place in her city. She used pictures, labels and complete sentences.

On showcase day, Samira stood beside her booklet. She felt nervous, but she remembered to breathe and speak slowly. 'Welcome to my English world,' she said. She explained her favourite page and answered two questions. Her classmates listened and gave kind feedback.

At the end, Samira wrote one final reflection: 'I can read, write and speak with more confidence now. Next year, I want to read longer stories by myself.' She closed her folder proudly. Her work was not perfect, but it showed how much she had learned.
readings.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

readings.title: My English World Project Brief
readings.passageScript:
Your capstone has four parts. First, choose three strong pieces from your Grade 2 English work. Select pieces that show different skills, such as reading, vocabulary, speaking or writing. Second, create a six-page illustrated booklet called My English World. Introduce yourself and include ideas from at least four earlier units. Third, record or present a two-minute talk about your booklet. Speak clearly, use complete sentences and answer one question. Finally, complete the self-assessment and write one goal for your next year of English.

Before you submit, check your work. Make sure your ideas are in a clear order. Use accurate vocabulary, capital letters and end punctuation. Read every page aloud. Ask a partner or teacher for one kind, specific suggestion, then improve your project. Your capstone should show what you can do now, not only what you can remember.
readings.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

readings.title: Showcase Day Dialogue
readings.passageScript:
Teacher: Welcome to our English showcase, Selma. What will you present?
Selma: I will present my booklet, My English World.
Teacher: Which page are you most proud of?
Selma: I am most proud of my plant page because I used labels and complete sentences.
Teacher: Please read one sentence for us.
Selma: 'The roots hold the plant in the soil, and the leaves help it use sunlight.'
Classmate: Your voice was clear. Can you tell us how you improved the page?
Selma: First, I checked my spelling. Then my partner asked me to add what the roots do. I revised the sentence.
Teacher: What is your next English goal?
Selma: I will practise reading longer stories aloud.
Teacher: That is a clear goal. Well done, Selma.
readings.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

grammar.title: Pronouns and Likes Review
grammar.explanation: Use he, she and they to avoid repeating names. Use like with I, you, we and they; use likes with he or she.
grammar.ruleAndExamples: Samira likes reading. She shares a story. Her friends like the city page. They ask questions.
grammar.commonMistake: Check that the grammar pattern matches the subject, time and meaning of the sentence.
grammar.memoryTip: Read the complete sentence aloud and compare it with the model.
grammar.practice: Write four linked sentences introducing two people and what they like.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

grammar.title: Actions Happening Now
grammar.explanation: Use am, is or are plus an action ending in -ing for actions happening now.
grammar.ruleAndExamples: I am presenting. Selma is reading. The learners are listening.
grammar.commonMistake: Check that the grammar pattern matches the subject, time and meaning of the sentence.
grammar.memoryTip: Read the complete sentence aloud and compare it with the model.
grammar.practice: Describe six actions happening during a project showcase.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

grammar.title: Clear Questions
grammar.explanation: Use What, Who, Where, How and How many to ask for different information.
grammar.ruleAndExamples: What is your topic? Who helped you? Where is the plant? How did you improve it?
grammar.commonMistake: Check that the grammar pattern matches the subject, time and meaning of the sentence.
grammar.memoryTip: Read the complete sentence aloud and compare it with the model.
grammar.practice: Write and answer one question with each of the five question patterns.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

grammar.title: There Is, There Are and Place Words
grammar.explanation: Use there is for one thing and there are for more than one. Add place words to show position.
grammar.ruleAndExamples: There is a booklet on the table. There are three pictures beside it.
grammar.commonMistake: Check that the grammar pattern matches the subject, time and meaning of the sentence.
grammar.memoryTip: Read the complete sentence aloud and compare it with the model.
grammar.practice: Describe a project table in five sentences using there is, there are and place words.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

grammar.title: Adjectives and Comparisons
grammar.explanation: Use adjectives to describe and -er than forms to compare suitable words.
grammar.ruleAndExamples: The moon is bright. A metre is longer than a centimetre. The final draft is clearer than the first draft.
grammar.commonMistake: Check that the grammar pattern matches the subject, time and meaning of the sentence.
grammar.memoryTip: Read the complete sentence aloud and compare it with the model.
grammar.practice: Write three descriptions and three accurate comparisons.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

grammar.title: Past Learning and Future Goals
grammar.explanation: Use past verbs to tell what happened and will to describe a future action.
grammar.ruleAndExamples: I wrote a poem. I measured a table. Next year, I will read longer books.
grammar.commonMistake: Check that the grammar pattern matches the subject, time and meaning of the sentence.
grammar.memoryTip: Read the complete sentence aloud and compare it with the model.
grammar.practice: Write three sentences about work you completed and three sentences about what you will do next.
grammar.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Read one capstone paragraph aloud. Pause at punctuation, speak clearly and reread one sentence more smoothly.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: Listen to my rehearsal. Give one specific strength and one age-appropriate improvement for clarity, vocabulary or sentence accuracy.
speaking.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

speaking.title: Speaking 4 - Explain My Best Page
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Explain what the page shows, which English skills it uses and how you improved it after feedback.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: Listen to my rehearsal. Give one specific strength and one age-appropriate improvement for clarity, vocabulary or sentence accuracy.
speaking.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

speaking.title: Speaking 5 - Rehearse and Answer Questions
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Give a two-minute rehearsal, then answer one What question and one How question from a partner.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: Listen to my rehearsal. Give one specific strength and one age-appropriate improvement for clarity, vocabulary or sentence accuracy.
speaking.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

speaking.title: Speaking 6 - Final Capstone Presentation
speaking.instructionsAndModelLines: Present your completed booklet and portfolio evidence clearly. Finish by stating one success and one next goal.
speaking.aiTutorPrompt: Listen to my rehearsal. Give one specific strength and one age-appropriate improvement for clarity, vocabulary or sentence accuracy.
speaking.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

writing.title: Writing 1 - Plan the Booklet
writing.promptAndInstructions: Choose at least four earlier units. Plan six pages with a topic, picture idea and two sentence ideas for each page.
writing.modelText: Page 1: About Me. Page 2: A Helpful Neighbour. Page 3: My Healthy Habit. Page 4: A Plant. Page 5: My Home. Page 6: A Place in My City.
writing.expectedLength: Complete the full stage as described
writing.successCriteria: I completed every instruction; used accurate Grade 2 vocabulary and grammar; organized ideas clearly; checked spelling, capitals and punctuation
writing.support: Use earlier Unit work, the curated review dictionary, models, sentence starters and teacher feedback.
writing.extension: Add one extra page, a labelled diagram or a connected paragraph with because, and or but.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

writing.title: Writing 2 - Build a Strong Sentence Bank
writing.promptAndInstructions: Write twelve accurate sentences for the planned pages. Include questions, descriptions, actions now, one past event and one future goal.
writing.modelText: I am presenting my favourite page. The tall tree is beside our home. Last term, I wrote a poem. Next year, I will read more stories.
writing.sentenceStarter: One sentence for my project is
writing.expectedLength: Complete the full stage as described
writing.successCriteria: I completed every instruction; used accurate Grade 2 vocabulary and grammar; organized ideas clearly; checked spelling, capitals and punctuation
writing.support: Use earlier Unit work, the curated review dictionary, models, sentence starters and teacher feedback.
writing.extension: Add one extra page, a labelled diagram or a connected paragraph with because, and or but.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

writing.title: Writing 3 - Write the First Draft
writing.promptAndInstructions: Write the complete six-page booklet. Each page needs a heading, two or more sentences and a matching illustration or visual.
writing.modelText: Welcome to my English world. I am Samira, and I like reading. This booklet shows the people, places and ideas I learned about this year.
writing.expectedLength: Six illustrated pages with at least 12 complete sentences
writing.successCriteria: I completed every instruction; used accurate Grade 2 vocabulary and grammar; organized ideas clearly; checked spelling, capitals and punctuation
writing.support: Use earlier Unit work, the curated review dictionary, models, sentence starters and teacher feedback.
writing.extension: Add one extra page, a labelled diagram or a connected paragraph with because, and or but.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

writing.title: Writing 4 - Revise with Feedback
writing.promptAndInstructions: Ask for one kind, specific suggestion. Improve the order, detail or vocabulary in at least three sentences and record what changed.
writing.modelText: First draft: The plant is nice. Revised: The young green plant has strong roots under the soil.
writing.expectedLength: Complete the full stage as described
writing.successCriteria: I completed every instruction; used accurate Grade 2 vocabulary and grammar; organized ideas clearly; checked spelling, capitals and punctuation
writing.support: Use earlier Unit work, the curated review dictionary, models, sentence starters and teacher feedback.
writing.extension: Add one extra page, a labelled diagram or a connected paragraph with because, and or but.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

writing.title: Writing 5 - Edit and Publish
writing.promptAndInstructions: Check capitals, spaces, spelling and punctuation. Read every page aloud and prepare a neat final version.
writing.modelText: Editing check: Every sentence begins with a capital letter, makes complete sense and ends with punctuation.
writing.expectedLength: Complete the full stage as described
writing.successCriteria: I completed every instruction; used accurate Grade 2 vocabulary and grammar; organized ideas clearly; checked spelling, capitals and punctuation
writing.support: Use earlier Unit work, the curated review dictionary, models, sentence starters and teacher feedback.
writing.extension: Add one extra page, a labelled diagram or a connected paragraph with because, and or but.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

writing.title: Writing 6 - Write the Final Reflection
writing.promptAndInstructions: Write six sentences about what you can do now, what was challenging, which evidence makes you proud and your next English goal.
writing.modelText: I am proud of my city page because I gave clear directions. Revising was challenging, but feedback helped me add detail. Next year, I will read longer stories aloud.
writing.expectedLength: Complete the full stage as described
writing.successCriteria: I completed every instruction; used accurate Grade 2 vocabulary and grammar; organized ideas clearly; checked spelling, capitals and punctuation
writing.support: Use earlier Unit work, the curated review dictionary, models, sentence starters and teacher feedback.
writing.extension: Add one extra page, a labelled diagram or a connected paragraph with because, and or but.
writing.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

activities.title: Activity 1 - Portfolio Treasure Hunt
activities.instructionsAndItems: Find one piece that shows vocabulary, one that shows reading, one that shows speaking and one that shows writing. Explain why each is useful evidence.
activities.answerSummary: Accept complete, relevant evidence that follows every step. Use the capstone rubric for quality and the teacher checklist for completion.
activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI, live session or classroom

activities.title: Activity 2 - Four-Unit Idea Map

activities.deliveryMode: Shared online UI, live session or classroom

activities.title: Activity 5 - Showcase Table Setup

assignments.title: My English World capstone portfolio
assignments.instructions: Submit the illustrated six-page booklet, three selected portfolio pieces, a two-minute oral presentation or recording, and the final self-assessment with one next English goal.
assignments.submissionType: Illustrated booklet + portfolio evidence + audio/video presentation + reflection
assignments.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: This is Samira. ___ is presenting her booklet.
quizzes.explanation: Use she for one girl or woman.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: The learners ___ listening carefully.
quizzes.explanation: Use are with the plural subject learners.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: Which question asks about a person?
quizzes.options: Who helped you? | What is your topic? | Where is the book? | How did you revise?
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: There ___ three pictures beside the booklet.
quizzes.explanation: Use there are for more than one thing.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: Which sentence gives a correct comparison?
quizzes.options: The metre is longer than the centimetre. | The metre is long than the centimetre. | The metre longer the centimetre. | The metre is longest than.
quizzes.correctAnswer: The metre is longer than the centimetre.
quizzes.explanation: Longer than is the correct comparison form.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: Which sentence describes an action happening now?
quizzes.options: Selma is reading. | Selma read yesterday. | Selma will read. | Selma likes books.
quizzes.explanation: Is plus reading describes an action happening now.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: Which sentence describes the past?
quizzes.options: I wrote a poem. | I will write a poem. | I am writing a poem. | I write poems.
quizzes.explanation: Wrote tells about a completed past action.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: Which sentence states a future goal?
quizzes.options: I will read longer stories. | I read a story. | I am reading now. | I wrote a story.
quizzes.explanation: Will expresses a future action or goal.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: What is the best feedback?
quizzes.options: Your details are clear; add punctuation to the last sentence. | It is bad. | Change everything. | I do not like it.
quizzes.correctAnswer: Your details are clear; add punctuation to the last sentence.
quizzes.explanation: Useful feedback is kind, specific and actionable.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

quizzes.question: What should happen before final submission?
quizzes.options: Revise, edit and read the project aloud. | Hide the first draft. | Remove every picture. | Skip the reflection.
quizzes.correctAnswer: Revise, edit and read the project aloud.
quizzes.explanation: A final check improves clarity and accuracy.
quizzes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

liveSessions.title: Capstone launch and portfolio treasure hunt
liveSessions.beforeSession: Bring your Grade 2 English folder or saved work.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min welcome; 10 min capstone model; 15 min portfolio treasure hunt; 10 min evidence choices; 5 min reflection
liveSessions.afterSession: Confirm three portfolio pieces and four earlier Unit themes.
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

liveSessions.title: Booklet planning and vocabulary retrieval
liveSessions.beforeSession: Complete Writing 1 and choose 12 review words.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min retrieval; 10 min idea-map modelling; 15 min page planning; 10 min vocabulary conference; 5 min next step
liveSessions.afterSession: Finish the six-page plan and sentence bank.
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

liveSessions.title: Grammar review and first-draft clinic
liveSessions.beforeSession: Complete Grammar Practices 1-3 and begin the draft.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min warm-up; 15 min grammar repair; 15 min guided drafting; 5 min partner read-aloud; 5 min feedback
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

liveSessions.title: Revision and peer feedback conference
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min model revision; 10 min Two Stars and One Next Step; 15 min revision; 10 min editing check; 5 min goal
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

liveSessions.title: Presentation rehearsal and questions
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min voice warm-up; 10 min teacher model; 15 min timed rehearsals; 10 min question practice; 5 min personal target
liveSessions.afterSession: Record Speaking 5 and apply one improvement.
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

liveSessions.title: My English World showcase
liveSessions.beforeSession: Bring the final booklet, portfolio pieces and reflection draft.
liveSessions.agenda: 5 min welcome; 25 min presentations; 5 min audience feedback; 5 min self-assessment; 5 min celebration and next goal
liveSessions.afterSession: Submit the booklet, recording, portfolio evidence and final reflection.
liveSessions.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

teacherNotes.note: Treat the capstone as supported independent performance. Confer briefly with each learner, preserve evidence of their own language, and give feedback that identifies one strength and one manageable next step.
teacherNotes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion

teacherNotes.note: Allow oral rehearsal, sentence frames, enlarged print, scribing where appropriate, alternative presentation formats and home-language planning. Assess the Grade 2 English evidence, not artistic skill or access to materials.
teacherNotes.origin: Ehel English Content Template v1.1 scalable conversion
